Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

The d_u.d_alias only exists in a specific kernel version, i.e. 3.2.0. The latter kernel version move it out of d_du again. So we have to update the comment message. And I think we have to create a LU- ticket to push the patch.

(1 comment)

I ran into this problem on Debian Wheezy Kernel 3.2 and ./configure --disable-server

make[3]: Entering directory `/usr/src/linux-headers-3.2.0-4-amd64'

Patch Set 6:

The reason I reversed this test was that the progression of the change in the upstream kernel was d_alais  list -> hlist then the move to d_u.d_alias. So in the case of d_u.d_alias it will be always a hlist. Now in the original test it will config it as using list instead of hlist since d_alias doesn't exist since it was moved to d_u union. I have a RHEL7 tree setup so I will look to see why it fails.
Uploaded patch set 7.

Patch Set 7:

Changed lustre-core.m4 to

root@[SSH]lustre-wheezy-testing:~/lustre-release>grep -C 10  "i_dentry_d_alias_list" lustre/autoconf/lustre-core.m4

#
# LC_HAVE_DENTRY_D_ALIAS_HLIST
#
# 3.6 switch i_dentry/d_alias from list to hlist
#
AC_DEFUN([LC_HAVE_DENTRY_D_ALIAS_HLIST], [
tmp_flags="$EXTRA_KCFLAGS"
EXTRA_KCFLAGS="-Werror"
LB_CHECK_COMPILE([if 'i_dentry/d_alias' uses 'list'],
i_dentry_d_alias_list, [
	#include <linux/fs.h>
	#include <linux/list.h>
],[
	struct inode inode;
        struct dentry dentry;
        INIT_LIST_HEAD(&dentry.d_alias);
        INIT_LIST_HEAD(&inode.i_dentry);
],[
	AC_DEFINE(HAVE_DENTRY_D_ALIAS_LIST, 1,
		[have i_dentry/d_alias uses list])


root@[SSH]lustre-wheezy-testing:~/lustre-release>sh ./autogen.sh && configure --disable-server

Still got this error:

 CC [M]  /root/lustre-release/lustre/llite/dcache.o
/root/lustre-release/lustre/llite/dcache.c: In function ‘ll_invalidate_aliases’:
/root/lustre-release/lustre/llite/dcache.c:297:2: error: ‘struct list_head’ has no member named ‘first’
/root/lustre-release/lustre/llite/dcache.c:297:2: error: initialization from incompatible pointer type [-Werror]
cc1: all warnings being treated as errors

Patch Set 7:

What I need to see is your config.log since the list test is failing. I grabbed a 3.2 wheezy kernel and built it on Ubuntu 14.04 and a Debian jesse box with no problems. Only by your config.log will I be able to fix this. Its some strange edge case.

Patch Set 7:

Ah so I see the problem. In the upstream kernel d_alias went from list to hlist. Then it was moved to d_u union. Most distros only have hlist in the d_u like what happened upstream. I was wondering if some distro had a setup that never existed upstream. Well that distro is debian :-( I can work around this stupidity.

Patch Set 7:

As a note this special setup exist on wheezy debian only if you enable genksym. I did so it didn't show up for me. Now I can reproduce the problem.
Uploaded patch set 8.

parents.subject: LU-6323 build: fix debian to compile with external IB

commit.subject: LU-6428 llite: Add check of d_alias and d_child
commit.message:
LU-6428 llite: Add check of d_alias and d_child

In some specific kernel versions like debian wheezy 3.2 or Ubuntu
14.04, d_alias has been moved to d_u.d_alias and d_u.d_child has
been moved to d_child in struct dentry in <linux/dcache.h>. This
patch add a check of this two members to make the llite be
compatible with those versions.

Signed-off-by: Wu Libin <lwu@ddn.com>
Signed-off-by: James Simmons <uja.ornl@yahoo.com>
Change-Id: I8ae63fdd887609e2049ac5e90f2bf4ff2ab14fd4
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/14060
Reviewed-by: Bob Glossman <bob.glossman@intel.com>
Tested-by: Jenkins
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Thomas Stibor <t.stibor@gsi.de>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
